title: Aurora Cannabis (ACB) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Aurora reported its strongest fiscal year ever with net revenue rising 21% on trailing 12-month basis. Company achieved major milestone by generating positive adjusted EBITDA for all four quarters of fiscal 2024, totaling $12.8 million annually - first in company history. Q4 metrics: Revenue $67.4 million (up 5% YoY), Adjusted gross margin 49%, Adjusted EBITDA $1.9 million, Cash position ~$180 million. Medical Cannabis represents 68% of revenue and 90% of gross profit. Global medical cannabis grew 20% in Q4. Canadian medical up nearly 10% due to insured patient sales. International medical surged nearly 40%, driven by Australia acquisition. Consumer Cannabis revenue declined to $10.2 million from $14.5 million as company shifted focus to higher-margin medical products. Plant Propagation (Bevo) revenue $10.4 million, down slightly from $10.8 million due to seasonal timing. Aurora acquired full ownership of MedReleaf Australia in February, positioning itself as #2 in rapidly growing AUD 400 million market. Company obtained TGA GMP certification for Canadian facilities, enabling product expansion into gummies, oils, and resin cartridges. Medical cannabis adjusted gross margin reached 66% - company's highest ever - benefiting from manufacturing efficiency and sustainable cost reductions. Q1 FY2025 Expectations: Consolidated revenue positioned for mid- to high teens growth versus Q4. Positive adjusted EBITDA expected to exceed Q4 levels. Operating cash flow improvements anticipated. Management emphasized medical-focused business model combined with EU GMP-certified manufacturing capabilities positions company advantageously for emerging opportunities globally.
